name: flutter-security
description: Performs comprehensive security audit to identify vulnerabilities and ensure secure coding practices

purpose.overview: Executes a thorough security audit of your Flutter project, scanning for hardcoded secrets, insecure storage practices, missing input validation, network security issues, and compliance with OWASP Mobile Top 10 vulnerabilities. Helps identify security risks before they reach production.
useCases: After implementing authentication or payment features
As part of compliance requirements (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS)
Before security audits or penetration testing

fixSuggestions.description: Include detailed remediation code examples for each finding

owaspOnly.description: Only check for OWASP Mobile Top 10 vulnerabilities

projectPath.description: Absolute path to the Flutter project to analyze. If not provided, uses the current workspace directory.

steps.name: Secret Detection
steps.description: Scans all Dart files for hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys, and credentials using regex pattern matching

steps.name: Secure Storage Check
steps.description: Verifies sensitive data uses flutter_secure_storage instead of SharedPreferences or plain text storage

steps.name: Input Validation Check
steps.description: Identifies TextFormField widgets and verifies validator functions exist to prevent injection attacks

steps.name: Network Security Check
steps.description: Identifies HTTP clients and verifies certificate pinning for production endpoints, checks for insecure HTTP usage

steps.name: Permission Check
steps.description: Parses AndroidManifest.xml and Info.plist to verify appropriate permission handling and justification

steps.name: OWASP Mobile Top 10 Check
steps.description: Validates against OWASP Mobile Top 10 vulnerabilities (M1-M10)

steps.name: Report Generation
steps.description: Compiles findings with severity levels, file locations, line numbers, remediation guidance, and OWASP references

privateKeys: RSA Private Key: -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
SSH Private Key: -----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
PGP Private Key: -----BEGIN PGP PRIVATE KEY BLOCK-----
falsePositiveHandling: Test files (ending in _test.dart) are flagged but marked as lower priority
Comments containing secrets are flagged but marked as potential false positives
Example code in documentation is flagged but marked as informational
Environment variable placeholders (e.g., YOUR_API_KEY_HERE) are excluded

M1.name: Improper Platform Usage
M1.description: Misuse of platform features or failure to use platform security controls

M2.name: Insecure Data Storage
M2.description: Insecure storage of sensitive data on device

M3.name: Insecure Communication
M3.description: Poor handshaking, incorrect SSL versions, weak negotiation, cleartext transmission

M5.name: Insufficient Cryptography
M5.description: Weak encryption algorithms, poor key management

relatedCommands.description: Run comprehensive verification including security checks
Coordinate multiple assistants for security improvements

relatedAssistants.description: Provides guidance on secure architecture patterns

requirements: Requirement 9.1: Scan all source files for hardcoded API keys and secrets
Requirement 9.2: Check for secure storage usage via flutter_secure_storage
Requirement 9.3: Verify input validation is present for user inputs
Requirement 9.4: Verify certificate pinning for sensitive API calls
Requirement 9.5: Verify appropriate permission handling for platform features
Requirement 9.6: Check against OWASP Mobile Top 10 vulnerabilities
Requirement 9.7: Generate security report with severity levels and remediation steps
notes: Secret detection uses regex patterns which may produce false positives. Always review findings in context
Some OWASP Mobile Top 10 checks (M6, M8, M9, M10) require runtime analysis or manual review and are not fully automated
Security scanning is static analysis only. Dynamic testing and penetration testing are still recommended
Certificate pinning verification checks for package usage but cannot verify pin correctness without runtime testing
Permission checks verify presence but cannot determine if permissions are actually needed without usage analysis
For production deployments, aim for a security score of 90+ with zero critical findings
Regular security audits should be conducted, especially after major feature additions or before releases
Consider integrating this command into CI/CD pipeline as a quality gate
Sensitive data includes: passwords, tokens, API keys, credit card numbers, SSNs, health records, biometric data
Follow principle of least privilege for permissions - only request what's absolutely necessary
